title: Lettre d'intention
text_preview: LETTRE D'INTENTION ENTRE LE GOUVERNEMENT DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE FRANÇAISE ET LE GOUVERNEMENT DE L'AUSTRALIE EN VUE D'UNE COOPÉRATION BILATÉRALE EN MATIÈRE DE CYBERSÉCURITÉ 1 LETTRE D'INTENTION La présente Lettre d'intention est établie entre les agences opérationnelles respectives en charge de la cyber sécurité pour le Gouvernement de la République française (ci-après dénommé la «France»), et le Gouvernement de l'Australi…

pages.text:
LETTRE D'INTENTION

ENTRE

LE GOUVERNEMENT DE LA RÉPUBLIQUE FRANÇAISE

ET

LE GOUVERNEMENT DE L'AUSTRALIE

EN VUE

D'UNE COOPÉRATION BILATÉRALE EN MATIÈRE DE

CYBERSÉCURITÉ

1

OBJET DE LA PRÉSENTE LETTRE D'INTENTION

La présente Lettre d'intention a pour objet de promouvoir une vision commune pou renforcer la coopération et la compréhension mutuelle entre la France et l'Australie dans le cadre de l'agenda international en matière de cybersécurité tel que mentionné par la

Déclaration conjointe. Elle ne vise pas à créer d'engagement ou de liens à valeur légale ou exécutoire entre la France et l'Australie.

La présente Lettre d'intention est signée en double exemplaire en langues française et anglaise.

Pour le Gouvernement de la République française

: Pour le Gouvernement de l'Australie

Signature

Wubrian

Nom

Nom

Date

Ministre de l'Euro, sel des Affaires itingzis 2 mai 2018

2.mат 2018

LETTER OF INTENT

BETWEEN

THE GOVERNMENT OF THE FRENCH REPUBLIC

AND

THE GOVERNMENT OF AUSTRALIA

FOR

BILATERAL COOPERATION IN CYBER SECURITY

LETTER OF INTENT

This Letter of Intent is made between the respective operational agencies in charge of cyber

RECOGNIZING that

The pace and development of emerging technologies, in conjunction with greater access to such technologies, is delivering enormous opportunities for both economic and social development.

However, like many countrics, France and Australia are concerned by the increased willingness of State and non-state actors to pursue their objectives by undertaking malicious cyber activities. As dependence on global information and communication technology networks increases, the potential damage of disruption caused by malicious cyber activities, whether by terrorists, criminal networks, state actors or their proxies, is significant and growing.

Just as cyber challenges are global, so too must our responses be. Techniques and procedures to discourage, mitigate and face malicious cyber activity are more effective if States cooperate to face common threats to cyber security.

NOTING that

The Joint Statement of Strategic Partnership between France and Australia, dated 3 May 2017 (Joint Statement"), reasserts the importance of close bilateral cooperation to address major international security issues of common concern, including cyber security.

The Joint Statement calls for cooperation in the framework of the international cyber security agenda.

CONSIDERING that

The cooperation on the Future Submarine Program ("FSP" established under the Framework

Agreement hetween the Government of Australia and the Government of the French Republic

Concerning Cooperation on the Future Submarine Program signed on 20 December 2016), vill further increase the existing strong defence relationship and recognises the importance of neasures to protect the security of information and communication systems underpinning the

FSP Program.

2

STATING that

Mutual understanding by the Participants of their respective national cyber security organisations facilitates establishing and maintaining strong cooperation in this field

The efficacy of cyber security responses depends on the timeliness and quality of information shared between relevant national bodies of States.

Strong international cooperation and experience-sharing is paramount to the development of effective national cyber security capabilities.

A relationship of trust between allies is necessary to efficiently discourage, mitigate and face malicious cyber activity,

SEEK to

Maintain and strengthen the mutual understanding and knowledge of their respective national cyber security organisations and policies, through exchanging updates on significant advancements in the Participants' organisations and policies.

Cooperate to share information concerning anticipated and ongoing cyber security threats and incidents.

Cooperate to share information concerning the evaluation of cyber security incident severity.

Enhance the ongoing cooperation on security of the information and communication systems underpinning the FSP Program.

Foster information exchanges through the development of bilateral secure communication capabilities as required.

Engage in regular exchanges of experiences regarding cyber security threats and incidents, as well as corresponding mitigation measures.

looperate to share information on laws, regulations. policies and practices concerning critica lational information infrastructure protection.

Cooperate to share information on laws, regulations, policies and practices, including industry-sponsored measures, concerning the certification of information and communication systems security products and services.

Cooperate to share information on training and guidance initiatives, including initiatives intended to improve public and academic understanding in the field of cyber security.

3

PURPOSE OF THIS LETTER OF INTENT

The purpose of this Letter of Intent is to promote a common vision 1o enhance cooperation and understanding between France and Australia in the framework of the international cyber security agenda as called for by the Joint Statement. This Letter of Intent is not intended to create legal or enforceable underakings or relationships between France and Australia.

This Letter of Intent is signed in duplicate in both the English and French language.

For the Government of the French Republic

For the Government of Australia
